description: Apply a single CARES/PAD systems-of-care revision to one existing live question record in place while preserving record ID, pool state, reviews, and learner history.

fields.citation: Dezfulian C, Cabañas JG, Buckley JR, et al. Part 4: Systems of Care: 2025 American Heart Association Guidelines for Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation and Emergency Cardiovascular Care. Circulation. 2025;152(suppl 2):S353-S384. doi:10.1161/CIR.0000000000001378. See community CPR, telecommunicator CPR, and public access defibrillation recommendations at S364-S367 and S375.

options.A: Increase epinephrine protocol compliance through medication checklists and post-call feedback for every cardiac arrest.
options.B: Build community cardiopulmonary resuscitation training, dispatcher-assisted cardiopulmonary resuscitation, and public automated external defibrillator access before ambulance arrival.
options.C: Buy mechanical cardiopulmonary resuscitation devices for all ambulances and track device deployment time as the main outcome.
options.D: Prioritize early advanced airway placement in all arrests and report first-pass success as the main system measure.

fields.rationale: For patient-oriented cardiac arrest outcomes, the strongest system lever in this scenario is earlier bystander cardiopulmonary resuscitation and defibrillation before EMS arrival. A may improve process reliability but has less direct evidence for the stated gap. C and D may be reasonable operational metrics in some systems but do not address the low bystander intervention and early defibrillation problem shown in CARES data.
fields.stem: A jurisdiction participates in the Cardiac Arrest Registry to Enhance Survival (CARES). Which initiative is most likely to improve patient-oriented outcomes?
fields.title: Community Intervention For CARES Outcomes

updates.position_statement: 2025 American Heart Association systems-of-care guidance for community CPR, telecommunicator CPR, and public access defibrillation
updates.reason: Single-question revision supplied 2026-07-05: update the CARES/PAD item to use current AHA systems-of-care guidance and patient-oriented outcome framing.
